figures.flow_path: Pump → T-mixer → Reactor Coil → Electrochemistry (Cathode/Anode) → Back Pressure Regulator → Sample Loop
figures.key_information: System includes electrochemical cell with cathode and anode, CSTR, chip reactor, and gas addition capability via mass flow controller
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Legend of flow reactor equipment. | Figure 2.  (Top) Commercial E-series reactor equipped with UV-150 photoreactor supplied by Vapourtec. (Bottom) LED panel inside a PFA coil (volume of 5 mL) showing the short pathlength achieved in flow.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Review paper discussing general limitations of batch photochemistry including heat dissipation, light penetration, and scalability issues
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Flow photochemistry enables better light penetration, improved heat dissipation, safer handling of hazardous reagents, and continuous operation for scalability
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor design changed from batch vessels to continuous flow reactors with LED light sources
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Photochemical reaction principles and light-driven mechanisms remain the same
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Improved safety through continuous processing of hazardous reagents and better temperature control

figure_descriptions: Multiple reactor schemes showing various flow photochemistry setups with LEDs, pumps, mixers, and back pressure regulators
Equipment diagrams showing commercial flow reactors and LED arrangements
Process flow diagrams for specific photochemical transformations

conflicts: Multiple different reactor configurations and conditions shown across figures - this is a review paper covering various systems rather than a single optimized process
notes: This is a review paper covering multiple flow photochemistry applications rather than a single batch-to-flow translation study. The extracted data represents general examples from the review rather than a specific optimized process comparison.
